body.title: CLOSED: Add FTS to palace + memory_search
body.result: FTS already in place: palace_fts_gin GIN index + palace_search already used plainto_tsquery. Real gap was memory_search using LIKE on 114K rows — upgraded to FTS hitting idx_memories_narrative_tsvector. abea27c
body.description:
palace.sessions.* nodes accumulate after every session but are not searchable by content — only by path/title. Hermes Agent's #1 ranking is partly attributed to its FTS layer over past sessions; our analogue is Postgres full-text search over adc.palace.

Add a tsvector column + GIN index to adc.palace.content. Update memory_search() to use the FTS index when a content query is provided.

**Affected files:** ADC migration script (new: scripts/migrations/YYYYMMDD_palace_fts.sql); wild_igor/igor/tools/memory_palace.py (memory_search update)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (Postgres FTS only); migration must be backward-compatible (add column, don't alter existing)
**Scope boundary:** In: tsvector column on adc.palace, GIN index, memory_search() updated to use FTS. Out: pgvector embeddings, relevance tuning beyond ts_rank, cross-table search.
**Test plan:** psql query using to_tsvector/plainto_tsquery returns expected rows after migration. memory_search() test: verify FTS path taken when query contains content keyword.
